response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong web, mobile, and backend experience, but the role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, deep-learning workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and embedded mentions do not approach the hardware architecture depth required for a Principal Performance Architect position. Educational background (BS in CS) without the 8+ years in the specific hardware/performance domain further misaligns with requirements.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering experience across multiple stacks and domains
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, testing, workshops)
Some low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby with high-throughput micro-services
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator hardware experience
No deep neural network training/inference optimization background
No hardware-software co-design or SIMD/MIMD architecture work shown

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN optimization background
Lacks performance analysis at hardware level
